sections.heading: What is a Pregnancy Weight Gain Calculator?
sections.content:
Our Pregnancy Weight Gain Calculator is a free tool designed to help expectant mothers estimate their ideal weight gain during pregnancy. It uses guidelines from the World Health Organization (WHO), Institute of Medicine (IOM), and NHS.

⚠️ Disclaimer: This tool is for educational purposes only. Always consult your doctor, gynecologist, or midwife for personalized guidance.

subSections.heading: Supports Fetal Growth
subSections.content: Ensures healthy development of the baby.

subSections.heading: Reduces Complications
subSections.content: Helps lower risks of gestational diabetes and high blood pressure.

subSections.heading: Maintains Maternal Health
subSections.content: Supports the mother’s health and recovery after delivery.

subSections.heading: Twin/Multiple Pregnancy
subSections.content: Higher ranges recommended — consult your doctor.

sections.heading: How to Use the Pregnancy Weight Gain Calculator (Step-by-Step)

subSections.heading: Step 1
subSections.content: Enter your height and weight (kg/cm or lbs/inches).

subSections.heading: Step 2
subSections.content: Select your pre-pregnancy BMI category (or let the calculator auto-compute).

subSections.heading: Step 3
subSections.content: Input your current week of pregnancy (1–40 weeks).

subSections.heading: Step 4
subSections.content: Click Calculate → Instantly see your recommended weight gain range and chart.

subSections.heading: Step 5
subSections.content: View trimester-specific guidance and weekly tracking.

subSections.heading: Global + Local Support
subSections.content: Works in kg/cm (Asia, EU) and lbs/inches (US, UK).

subSections.heading: Trimester Tracking
subSections.content: Provides week-by-week pregnancy weight gain insights.

subSections.heading: Guideline-Based
subSections.content: Follows WHO, IOM, and NHS recommendations.

subSections.heading: Supports All BMIs
subSections.content: Works for underweight, normal, overweight, and obese mothers.

faq.q: How much weight should I gain during pregnancy?
faq.a: It depends on your pre-pregnancy BMI. On average, women with normal BMI gain 11–16 kg (25–35 lbs).

faq.q: How do I calculate pregnancy weight gain?
faq.a: Use this calculator by entering your height, weight, BMI, and current pregnancy week.

faq.q: Why is pregnancy weight gain important?
faq.a: It ensures your baby’s healthy growth and reduces risks of complications like premature birth or gestational diabetes.

faq.q: What is safe pregnancy weight gain per week?
faq.a:
First trimester: 1–2 kg (2–5 lbs) total.
Second & third trimesters: about 0.5 kg (1 lb) per week.

faq.q: Can I use this calculator for twins or multiples?
faq.a: Yes, but multiple pregnancies require special care. Consult your doctor for personalized advice.
Yes, it follows WHO and NHS guidelines and works globally (South Asia, Europe, US, etc.).
